title: Rename “For Organizations” to “Subscription plans” and add a Subscription Plans page

requirements.text: Update the landing page final CTA button label from “For Organizations” to “Subscription plans” and make it navigable (clickable) to a new Subscription Plans view/page.

quotes: at the end of the page there is a option named "for organizations" change to "Subscription plans" and after we click on that take us to page of subscription plans

acceptanceCriteria: On the landing page near the end (Final CTA section), the secondary CTA button text reads exactly “Subscription plans”.
Clicking “Subscription plans” navigates the user to a Subscription Plans screen without a full page reload.
Navigation does not break the existing “Start MindVault” / experience flow.

requirements.text: Create a new Subscription Plans page/screen that clearly displays: Single session ₹200; ₹800 for 4 sessions in a month; ₹1600 for 12 sessions in 3 months; and a prominent note that the first session is entirely free.

quotes: starting from a single session 200 rupees , 800 rupees for 4 sessions in a month and 1600 rupees for 12 sessions  in 3 months. furthermore the first session will be entirely free

acceptanceCriteria: The Subscription Plans page shows three plans with prices exactly: “₹200”, “₹800”, and “₹1600”, and includes the corresponding session/time descriptions.
The page includes user-facing text that communicates: “The first session is entirely free.”
The Subscription Plans page provides a clear way to return to the landing page (e.g., a “Go back” control).

constraints: Do not edit any files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only).
Do not modify immutable hook/entry files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x)).
Keep the backend as a single Motoko actor; no backend changes are required unless strictly necessary for this UI-only request.
nonGoals: Implementing payments, checkout, or Stripe integration.
Adding account/session tracking for plan purchases.
Creating organization-specific pricing or flows beyond the requested rename and subscription plans display.
